summary: Small single-story rectangular building with a recessed central roof void. Walls are brick with stone-corner pillars, a lower wooden plinth, and a band of white blocks below a dark brick (nether) trim; surrounded by a raised white slab/parapet walk and set inside a shallow dirt moat.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with inner recessed roof opening (courtyard-like void).
shape.symmetry: roughly symmetric on long axis; appears symmetric on short axis as well

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: possible window sill or interior step

elements: continuous row windows along visible facades (3 windows on each long side in view)
stone corner pillars at each facade edge
white band below nether brick trim (possibly quartz blocks)
raised white outer border/parapet around building (quartz slabs or blocks)
recessed central roof area filled with dark nether bricks
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular 9x13 footprint (estimate) with walls 4 blocks tall of brick on top of a 1-block-high wooden plinth.
Place stonebrick blocks at all four vertical corners as 1-block-wide pillars reaching full wall height.
Add a continuous white band (quartz blocks likely) above the brick walls, then a 1-block-high dark nether brick band above that, capped with stone slabs as roof edge.
Leave a centered rectangular recessed roof opening (inner rectangle ~5x7) filled with nether brick floor and surrounded by the stone-slab cap.
Install horizontal window groups (glass panes) in the middle of the brick wall on long sides, with wooden slab or plank row directly above ground level as interior/threshold.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from perspective/top views; could be off by ±2 blocks.
White band material appears to be quartz blocks but could be white concrete or another white block.
Raised outer border around the building likely quartz slabs/blocks — exact block vs slab and thickness uncertain.
The dark band appears to be nether brick blocks; could be nether brick slabs or another dark brick variant.
Interior layout and whether there is an entrance door on a hidden face is not visible.
